title: French banking giant Crédit Agricole launches EURXT euro stablecoin
summary: French banking giant Crédit Agricole launches EURXT euro stablecoin
briefing: Crédit Agricole’s CACEIS launches a euro-pegged stablecoin on Ethereum with 20.02 million tokens issued, targeting institutional flows and tokenized fund access.

title: Crypto enters Q3 with thinner liquidity but less leverage after Q2 reset: Talos
summary: Crypto enters Q3 with thinner liquidity but less leverage after Q2 reset: Talos
briefing: Bitcoin and Ether open interest fell sharply after $8.35 billion in long liquidations, while ETF outflows, weaker Strategy purchases and declining market depth reduced liquidity.

title: Bitcoin ETFs lose record $4.5B in June, eclipsing Strategy's $1.25B raise
summary: Bitcoin ETFs lose record $4.5B in June, eclipsing Strategy's $1.25B raise
briefing: US spot Bitcoin ETFs logged a record $4.5 billion in June outflows, pushing year-to-date totals to $5.5 billion, signaling an unprecedented pace of withdrawals.

title: Former Goliath Ventures CEO pleads guilty in $400M crypto Ponzi case
summary: Former Goliath Ventures CEO pleads guilty in $400M crypto Ponzi case
briefing: Former Goliath Ventures CEO Christopher Delgado pleaded guilty to fraud and money laundering and agreed to forfeit properties, vehicles, luxury goods and crypto wallets.

title: Taiwan’s legislature passes crypto, stablecoin regulations
summary: Taiwan’s legislature passes crypto, stablecoin regulations
briefing: Taiwan’s legislative branch has passed the nation’s first crypto and stablecoin rules, which aim to integrate the country with the global crypto market.

title: Anthropic to bring back Fable 5 as US lifts export controls
summary: Anthropic to bring back Fable 5 as US lifts export controls
briefing: Claude Fable 5 will be redeployed with a new set of classifiers designed to identify and block a broader range of cybersecurity-related tasks.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Here’s what happened in crypto today
briefing: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Trump earned more from crypto than real estate in 2025, filings show
summary: Trump earned more from crypto than real estate in 2025, filings show
briefing: Memecoin sales and royalties made the most money for Trump last year, more than all of his golf courses combined.

title: Taiwan Passes Sweeping Crypto Law With Licensing, Stablecoin Rules
summary: Taiwan Passes Sweeping Crypto Law With Licensing, Stablecoin Rules
briefing: The new law puts virtual asset firms under FSC oversight for the first time and sets reserve-and-trust rules for stablecoins.

title: Goliath Ventures CEO Pleads Guilty to $250M Crypto Ponzi Scheme
summary: Goliath Ventures CEO Pleads Guilty to $250M Crypto Ponzi Scheme
briefing: Christopher Delgado’s "liquidity pool" fraud took in at least $400 million, with the proceeds spent on mansions, Lamborghinis, and Rolexes.

summary: Trump Discloses Over $1.2 Billion in Crypto Earnings, $50M in Bitcoin Holdings
briefing: The president’s annual financial disclosure report was released on Tuesday.

summary: Binance, Changpeng Zhao Sued for $200M by British Investors: Reuters
briefing: Crypto exchange Binance and its founder were sued for nearly $200 million in a new lawsuit filed in the United Kingdom.

briefing: The tokenized NYLIM Anemoy U.S. High Yield Corporate Bond Segregated Portfolio is launching in collaboration with Centrifuge.

title: Kalshi Sports Markets Banned in Michigan as Temporary Blackout Takes Effect
summary: Kalshi Sports Markets Banned in Michigan as Temporary Blackout Takes Effect
briefing: A Michigan judge granted a restraining order preventing the prediction market from offering sports-related wagers for the next 14 days.
